decomposition_notes: Multi-goal split along Phoenix layer seams identified from the requirements doc. The work spans three orthogonal seams: (1) server-side protocol & data layer — detecting goal completion atomically, returning after_goal hook metadata in the completion/mark_reviewed response, and gating goal Done transition; (2) agent-side hook execution — parsing the new ## after_goal section from .stride.md, executing the hook with GOAL_* env vars, reporting results back; (3) observability — telemetry counters, adoption metric, goal-to-Done latency SLO. Plus a fourth goal for the plugin/marketplace version bump release coordination. CLAIM ORDER: G1 (server protocol) must land first because G2 (agent execution) depends on receiving the new hook metadata shape; G3 (observability) depends on both G1 and G2 emitting the events it observes; G4 (release coordination) is claimed last after G1+G2 land in main. Within each goal, sibling tasks use array-index dependencies. Cross-goal sequencing lives here in decomposition_notes only.

goals.title: Add server-side after_goal hook detection and delivery protocol
goals.why: Goal-level work (consolidated PRs, single deploy promotions, single release notes) currently has no protocol-blessed home. Without server-side detection of 'last child task completed', agents cannot know which completion response should carry the after_goal hook metadata, and concurrent sibling completions risk firing the hook twice or skipping it entirely.
goals.what: Detect when a child-task completion finishes its parent goal under a transactional boundary, bundle after_goal hook metadata into that completion / mark_reviewed response alongside the existing four hooks, and gate the goal's transition to Done on the agent reporting the hook succeeded.
goals.where_context: Server-side completion and mark_reviewed endpoints; goal status transition logic; hook metadata serialization shared with the existing four hooks.
goals.description: This goal owns the server-side plumbing: race-free detection of 'this completion finishes the parent goal', extending the hook-metadata response shape with a fifth entry, and ensuring the goal cannot reach Done until the agent reports after_goal exited 0. Back-compat: an agent that does not report after_goal back (older agents) must still be able to complete goals — the server treats missing/empty after_goal as a no-op.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
Final child-task completion response includes after_goal hook metadata with name, timeout, blocking, env, execute_before, execute_after fields
Detection of 'last child → goal complete' is serialized with the child-task Done transition under one transactional boundary
Concurrent sibling completions cannot produce duplicate after_goal deliveries or skip after_goal entirely
Goal status transitions to Done only after agent reports after_goal exit_code == 0
If hook execution fails (non-zero exit), goal stays In Progress and is re-runnable
Older agents that don't send after_goal results can still complete goals (empty-hook back-compat path)
Integration test covers both needs_review=false (auto-Done after after_doing) and needs_review=true (Done after mark_reviewed) flows
pitfalls: Do not introduce a new endpoint for primary after_goal delivery — bundle into the existing completion / mark_reviewed response
Do not break the existing array-of-hooks shape that older agents parse
Do not skip the transactional boundary — race-free detection is the riskiest assumption in the doc

tasks.title: Add after_goal to the hook-metadata serializer alongside the existing four hooks
tasks.description: Extend the hook-metadata serializer used by completion and mark_reviewed responses so it can emit a fifth entry, after_goal, with the same field shape (name, timeout, blocking, env, execute_before, execute_after) as the existing four. The serializer must be safe to call when no after_goal is applicable (returns the existing four-hook payload unchanged for non-final-child completions).
tasks.why: Every downstream task in this goal depends on a serializer that can emit a fifth hook entry. Landing this first keeps later tasks small.
tasks.what: Add an after_goal branch to the hook-metadata builder; ensure non-final-child completions still emit the existing four-hook payload.
tasks.where_context: Hook metadata builder/serializer module used by the completion endpoint.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Serializer can produce after_goal metadata with the same field shape as the other four hooks
Non-final-child completion responses are byte-identical to the pre-change four-hook payload
Unit test covers both shapes (four-hook and five-hook)
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror the existing structure of the four existing hooks in the serializer
Do not invent a new field type — name, timeout, blocking, env, execute_before, execute_after only
pitfalls: Do not change field ordering or naming for the existing four hooks — older agents parse by name

key_files.note: Hook metadata serializer — add after_goal branch
Cover four-hook and five-hook payload shapes

unit_tests: Serializer emits four-hook payload when after_goal is not applicable
Serializer emits five-hook payload with after_goal when applicable
Field shape of after_goal matches the existing four hooks
edge_cases: Empty after_goal definition still serializes a valid (empty) entry

tasks.title: Add transactional last-child-completion detection for parent goal
tasks.description: Add a context function that, inside the same transaction as the child-task Done transition, determines whether the just-completed child is the final child of its parent goal. The check must be race-free under concurrent sibling completions — exactly one completion sees 'last child → goal complete' and is therefore eligible to receive after_goal in its response.
tasks.why: This is the riskiest assumption in the requirements doc: concurrent final-child completions must not produce duplicate after_goals or silently skip the hook. Solving it transactionally is the only safe answer.
tasks.what: Add Kanban.Tasks.finalize_child_and_check_goal_complete/2 (or equivalent context function) that wraps the child Done transition and the sibling-count check in one Ecto.Multi.
tasks.where_context: Tasks context module that owns the child-task Done transition.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Function runs the child Done transition and the 'is parent goal now complete?' check inside a single transaction
Returns {:ok, :last_child} or {:ok, :not_last_child} unambiguously
Concurrent sibling completions cannot both return :last_child (verified by a concurrency test)
Unit + concurrency test covers both branches
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Use Ecto.Multi for transactional composition
Follow existing context-function patterns in lib/kanban/tasks.ex
pitfalls: Do not perform the sibling-count check outside the transaction — that is the race
Do not use a separate advisory lock when row-level locking on the parent goal will do

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including the concurrency test

integration_tests: Two concurrent sibling completions — only one returns :last_child
edge_cases: Goal with a single child task
Goal with a child whose status flapped

tasks.title: Wire after_goal into completion endpoint response when last child detected
tasks.description: Update the completion endpoint (PATCH /api/tasks/:id/complete) so that when the transactional last-child check returns :last_child, the response payload includes after_goal hook metadata alongside the existing four hooks. Covers the needs_review=false path where a child task goes straight to Done after after_doing.
tasks.why: The completion endpoint is the primary delivery channel for after_goal in the needs_review=false flow.
tasks.what: Call the new context function, branch on :last_child, and add after_goal to the hook-metadata payload.
tasks.where_context: Completion endpoint controller and its response builder.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
PATCH /api/tasks/:id/complete returns after_goal in hook metadata when the completed child is the last child of a parent goal AND the goal is in needs_review=false flow
When the completed child is NOT the last child, response shape is unchanged
Integration test covers both branches
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror how the existing four hooks are added to the response
Do not call the serializer directly from the controller — go through the context
pitfalls: Do not duplicate the transactional check in the controller — call the context function from task 1

key_files.note: Completion endpoint — wire after_goal into response

edge_cases: Child has no parent goal — response is unchanged

tasks.title: Wire after_goal into mark_reviewed endpoint response when last child detected
tasks.description: Update PATCH /api/tasks/:id/mark_reviewed so that when the reviewed child is the last child of its parent goal (needs_review=true flow), the response includes after_goal hook metadata. This is the parallel path to the completion endpoint for tasks that require review.
tasks.why: The requirements doc explicitly calls out that 'goal is ready for after_goal exactly when all child tasks are in Done' covers BOTH needs_review=false (Done after after_doing) and needs_review=true (Done after mark_reviewed).
tasks.what: Add the same last-child / after_goal wiring to the mark_reviewed endpoint that task 2 added to the completion endpoint.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
PATCH /api/tasks/:id/mark_reviewed returns after_goal in hook metadata when the reviewed child is the last child of its parent goal
When the reviewed child is NOT the last child, response shape is unchanged
Integration test covers both branches in the needs_review=true flow
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Reuse the context function from task 1
Mirror the wiring added to the completion endpoint in task 2
pitfalls: Do not fork the after_goal logic — call the same context function used by the completion endpoint

key_files.note: mark_reviewed endpoint — wire after_goal into response

integration_tests: mark_reviewed of last child returns five-hook payload
mark_reviewed of non-last child returns four-hook payload

tasks.title: Gate goal Done transition on agent-reported after_goal success
tasks.description: Add an endpoint (or extend an existing one) for the agent to report after_goal execution results. While the goal has an outstanding after_goal whose result has not yet arrived OR whose last reported exit_code was non-zero, the goal remains In Progress. Only on a reported exit_code == 0 does the goal transition to Done. Back-compat: if the agent never reports back (older agent), the goal can still reach Done via the empty-hook path (decision: server treats missing report after a grace window as success, matching the requirements doc's 'treat missing ## after_goal as no-op').
tasks.why: The goal must not reach Done while after_goal has not yet succeeded — this is the core protocol guarantee of the feature.
tasks.what: Add the agent-result endpoint, gate the goal Done transition on its success, and implement the back-compat path for agents that don't report.
tasks.where_context: Goal status transition logic + new (or extended) agent-result endpoint.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Endpoint accepts {exit_code, output, duration_ms} from the agent and updates the goal's after_goal status
Goal stays In Progress while after_goal has not succeeded
Goal transitions to Done on reported exit_code == 0
Failed runs (non-zero exit) leave the goal in In Progress and re-runnable
Older agents that don't report still produce a Done transition via the back-compat path
Integration test covers success, failure, and back-compat branches
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror existing agent-result reporting patterns from the four existing hooks if any exist
Reuse the {exit_code, output, duration_ms} shape from the existing hook protocol
pitfalls: Do not auto-transition goals to Done on a non-zero exit — the doc is explicit that failure leaves goal in In Progress
Do not allow the same after_goal attempt to be reported twice with different exit codes — the latest report wins but must be auditable

unit_tests: Goal stays In Progress on non-zero exit
Goal transitions to Done on exit_code == 0
integration_tests: Full success flow: last-child completion → agent reports success → goal Done
Failure flow: agent reports non-zero → goal still In Progress → second report succeeds → goal Done
Back-compat: agent never reports → goal eventually Done via empty-hook path
edge_cases: Duplicate reports for the same attempt
Report arrives after goal already Done (idempotent)

goals.title: Add agent-side after_goal hook parsing and execution
goals.why: The server can deliver after_goal metadata, but until the agent-side code knows how to (a) parse the new ## after_goal section from .stride.md, (b) execute the hook with GOAL_* environment variables, and (c) report the result back to the new endpoint, the protocol is half-built.
goals.what: Extend the hook-execution skill / orchestrator to recognize the fifth hook, populate GOAL_* env vars, execute the user's command from .stride.md, and POST the result back.
goals.where_context: stride skills repo: hook execution orchestrator and .stride.md parser.
goals.description: This goal is agent-side only. It depends on G1's response shape landing in main so the agent has something real to receive.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
.stride.md parser recognizes a ## after_goal section and treats missing as empty no-op
Hook executor runs the after_goal command with GOAL_ID, GOAL_IDENTIFIER, GOAL_TITLE, GOAL_DESCRIPTION env vars set
Hook is blocking — agent waits for exit before reporting
Result {exit_code, output, duration_ms} is POSTed to the server's agent-result endpoint
Older .stride.md files without ## after_goal continue to work (no error)
Integration test exercises the full agent-side flow against a stubbed server response
pitfalls: Do not couple the executor to the PR-creation use case — the hook is general-purpose
Do not break the existing four-hook execution paths

tasks.title: Extend .stride.md parser to recognize ## after_goal section
tasks.description: Update the .stride.md parser used by the stride skills plugin to recognize a fifth hook section, ## after_goal, following the same single-section parsing rule as the existing four hooks. A missing section parses to an empty/no-op hook definition.
tasks.why: The agent needs to know what command to run when the server delivers after_goal metadata.
tasks.what: Add 'after_goal' to the parser's recognized section list; cover the missing-section case as no-op.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Parser recognizes ## after_goal alongside the four existing hooks
Missing section returns an empty/no-op definition (back-compat)
Multiple ## after_goal sections — only the first is used (matching existing single-section rule)
Unit test covers all three cases
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Mirror parsing of the four existing sections exactly — same single-section, first-wins rule
pitfalls: Do not change how the existing four sections are parsed

key_files.note: Document the new section and parsing rule

verification_steps.step_text: Author a .stride.md with ## after_goal and verify the parser surfaces the command; author one without the section and verify it parses to an empty no-op

tasks.title: Add GOAL_* environment variable population to hook executor
tasks.description: Extend the hook executor so that when it runs after_goal, it sets GOAL_ID, GOAL_IDENTIFIER, GOAL_TITLE, GOAL_DESCRIPTION (and the existing BOARD_* / COLUMN_* / AGENT_NAME / HOOK_NAME) in the child process environment, parallel to how TASK_* is set for the four existing hooks.
tasks.why: User commands in ## after_goal need access to goal-scoped metadata (e.g., to construct a PR title that names the goal).
tasks.what: Add the GOAL_* env var mapping in the executor; pull values from the server-supplied hook.env block.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
GOAL_ID, GOAL_IDENTIFIER, GOAL_TITLE, GOAL_DESCRIPTION are present in the after_goal child process environment
Values come from the server-supplied hook.env (no client-side lookup)
Existing TASK_* / BOARD_* / COLUMN_* / AGENT_NAME / HOOK_NAME pattern is followed
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror the TASK_* env var pattern exactly
Use the server-supplied env block as the source of truth
pitfalls: Do not invent GOAL_* values client-side — only forward what the server supplies

verification_steps.step_text: Run after_goal with a command that echoes GOAL_ID / GOAL_TITLE; verify the values match the goal that finished

manual_tests: Echo command verifies env vars at runtime
edge_cases: Server omits GOAL_DESCRIPTION — executor sets it to empty string, does not error

tasks.title: Execute after_goal as a blocking hook and capture result
tasks.description: Run the user's after_goal command as a blocking hook (agent waits for the child process to exit), capture exit_code, stdout/stderr output, and duration_ms. Apply the same timeout budget the existing blocking hooks use (60-120s window).
tasks.why: The goal cannot reach Done until after_goal succeeds — the executor must block on the command and report the precise result back to the server.
tasks.what: Add the blocking-execution path for after_goal, parallel to before_doing / after_doing.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Executor blocks on after_goal until child process exits or timeout elapses
Result is captured as {exit_code, output, duration_ms}
Timeout matches the server-supplied hook.timeout (within the 60-120s window)
Timeout produces a recognizable non-zero result so the server keeps the goal In Progress
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Mirror the existing blocking-hook execution path used by before_doing / after_doing
pitfalls: Do not silently swallow non-zero exits — they must be reported back
Do not exceed the server-supplied timeout

key_files.note: Document after_goal as blocking and how timeouts are honored

verification_steps.step_text: Run after_goal with a command that exits 0 (verify success result) and one that exits 1 (verify failure result); time both and confirm duration_ms is plausible

tasks.title: Report after_goal result back to server agent-result endpoint
tasks.description: After executing after_goal, POST {exit_code, output, duration_ms} to the server's agent-result endpoint added in G1. On success (exit_code == 0), the server transitions the goal to Done; on failure, the agent surfaces the failure to the user and the goal stays In Progress for re-run.
tasks.why: Without the report, the server cannot transition the goal to Done — the result reporting is what closes the protocol loop.
tasks.what: Add the HTTP POST to the agent-result endpoint and surface failures to the user.
tasks.where_context: stride skills repo hook executor / API client.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
POST sent to the agent-result endpoint with the correct payload shape
On 2xx response, agent considers the hook reported
On non-zero exit_code, agent surfaces the failure to the user (does not silently retry)
Network errors are retried with bounded backoff
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Use the same HTTP client and auth pattern as the existing four-hook reporting (if any)
Mirror the {exit_code, output, duration_ms} shape exactly
pitfalls: Do not retry on non-zero exit_code — the user must fix and re-run
Do not block the agent indefinitely on network failures — bounded backoff only

verification_steps.step_text: Run a full end-to-end goal-completion flow against a dev server and verify the goal transitions to Done after after_goal succeeds
verification_steps.expected_result: Goal moves to Done in the kanban UI

integration_tests: End-to-end goal completion with after_goal exit 0
End-to-end with after_goal exit 1 — goal stays In Progress, user re-runs, second attempt succeeds
manual_tests: Verify Done transition in kanban UI
edge_cases: Network drop during POST — bounded retry
Server returns 5xx — bounded retry then surface error

goals.title: Add after_goal observability (telemetry, adoption metric, latency SLO)
goals.why: The requirements doc names three measurable success signals: a protocol-plumbing telemetry counter (leading), an adoption metric (riskiest assumption — leading), and a goal-to-Done latency p95 SLO (lagging). Without instrumentation, the team cannot know whether the feature is succeeding on its own terms — and the doc explicitly flags that adoption stalling near zero is the premortem-selected failure mode.
goals.what: Emit a telemetry counter on every after_goal delivery, track adoption from after_goal-result reports, and measure goal-to-Done latency from final after_review completion to goal Done transition.
goals.description: All three metrics are server-side. Adoption is inferred from completion telemetry (per the doc's open question — this is the cleanest answer without requiring agents to upload .stride.md snapshots).
goals.acceptance_criteria:
Telemetry counter emitted on every after_goal delivery in completion / mark_reviewed responses
Adoption metric: count of distinct projects that have reported at least one non-empty after_goal result in a rolling 7-day window, exposed on the metrics dashboard
Goal-to-Done latency: p50/p95 measured server-side from final after_review/after_doing completion timestamp to goal Done transition timestamp, exposed on the metrics dashboard
Goals with no after_goal declaration are excluded from the latency metric
pitfalls: Do not measure latency on goals that never had an after_goal — that pollutes the SLO
Do not infer adoption from .stride.md uploads — agents do not send them per the open questions

tasks.title: Emit after_goal delivery telemetry event from completion endpoints
tasks.description: Add a :telemetry.execute call when the completion or mark_reviewed endpoint includes after_goal in its response. Event name follows the existing kanban telemetry convention.
tasks.why: Leading indicator — verifies the protocol plumbing works in production.
tasks.what: Emit [:kanban, :hooks, :after_goal, :delivered] with goal_id and project_id metadata.
tasks.where_context: Completion and mark_reviewed endpoints; telemetry handler module.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Event emitted exactly once per after_goal delivery
Event metadata includes goal_id and project_id
Unit test asserts the event is emitted
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Mirror existing :telemetry.execute calls in the codebase
pitfalls: Do not emit the event when the after_goal is the back-compat empty no-op — that pollutes the counter

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests including the telemetry assertion pass

unit_tests: Event NOT emitted on back-compat empty no-op

tasks.title: Add after_goal adoption metric (distinct projects reporting in 7-day window)
tasks.description: Add a metric that counts the number of distinct projects that have reported at least one non-empty after_goal result in the trailing 7 days. Surface it on the existing metrics dashboard.
tasks.why: Speaks directly to the riskiest assumption: if adoption stalls near zero, the lagging latency metric will have too few samples to matter.
tasks.what: Add a query in the metrics context and a tile/row on the metrics dashboard.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Metric counts distinct projects with at least one non-empty after_goal result in trailing 7 days
Metric visible on the existing metrics dashboard
Unit test covers the metric query
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Use the existing metrics context for the query
Use existing dashboard tile components
pitfalls: Do not count back-compat empty no-op reports as adoption — only non-empty after_goal results count

edge_cases: Same project reports multiple times — counted once

tasks.title: Add goal-to-Done latency p50/p95 metric for after_goal goals
tasks.description: Add a metric measuring the elapsed time between the final child task's after_doing/after_review completion timestamp and the parent goal's Done transition timestamp. Excludes goals that had no after_goal declaration. Surface p50 and p95 on the metrics dashboard.
tasks.why: Lagging SLO — verifies the feature is not stranding goals in In Progress.
tasks.what: Add a query that joins goal status transitions with final child completion timestamps and computes percentiles; surface on the dashboard.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Metric computes elapsed seconds from final child completion to goal Done
Goals with no after_goal declaration are excluded
p50 and p95 visible on the metrics dashboard
Unit test covers the percentile calculation
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Use the existing metrics context
Reuse existing percentile helpers if any exist
pitfalls: Do not include goals that finished via the back-compat empty no-op path — they would skew p95 toward zero
Do not use agent-reported duration_ms alone — the doc's open question prefers server-side timing

key_files.note: Cover percentile calculation and exclusion rule

edge_cases: Zero goals in window — metric returns nil, not crash

goals.title: Release after_goal hook in stride and stride-marketplace plugin repos
goals.why: Per the requirements doc constraints and the long-standing release rule, a plugin bump in the stride repo alone is not releasable — the stride-marketplace repo must also bump to publish to users. Without this coordinated release, agents in the wild will not pick up the new after_goal section parsing or the GOAL_* env vars.
goals.what: Bump version in both stride and stride-marketplace, update changelogs and skill docs to document the new hook, and tag releases.
goals.where_context: stride/ repo (skill plugin source) and stride-marketplace/ repo (marketplace publishing).
goals.description: Pure release-coordination goal. Claimed last, after G1 and G2 have landed in main.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
stride repo version bumped and changelog updated with after_goal entry
stride-marketplace repo version bumped and changelog updated
Skill documentation describes ## after_goal section, blocking semantics, and GOAL_* env vars
Both repos tagged at the new version
pitfalls: Do not release stride alone — marketplace bump is required for end-user pickup
Do not forget to document the back-compat behavior (missing section = no-op)

tasks.title: Update stride skill documentation to describe after_goal hook
tasks.description: Update the stride-workflow skill SKILL.md and related docs to describe the new ## after_goal section, its blocking semantics, GOAL_* env vars, back-compat behavior, and the canonical PR-creation example.
tasks.why: Without documentation, users cannot adopt the feature — and adoption is the riskiest assumption in the doc.
tasks.what: Add after_goal to the hooks table, document the env vars, add an example .stride.md snippet.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Hooks table includes after_goal with timing, blocking, timeout columns
GOAL_* env vars documented alongside TASK_* / BOARD_* / COLUMN_*
Back-compat behavior documented (missing section = no-op)
Canonical PR-creation example included
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Mirror the existing four-hook documentation structure exactly
pitfalls: Do not couple documentation to the PR-creation use case — note that the hook is general-purpose

key_files.note: Add after_goal to hooks table and document env vars

verification_steps.step_text: Read the updated SKILL.md end-to-end and verify the hooks table, env vars, back-compat, and example are all present and correct

tasks.title: Bump stride repo version and update changelog
tasks.description: Bump the stride repo version (semver minor for a new feature) and add a changelog entry describing the after_goal hook addition.
tasks.why: The skill plugin source repo must be versioned before the marketplace can pick it up.
tasks.what: Update version metadata and CHANGELOG.md in the stride repo.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Version bumped to next minor
CHANGELOG.md entry describes after_goal, GOAL_* env vars, and back-compat
Commit tagged at the new version
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Mirror the format of previous CHANGELOG entries in the stride repo
pitfalls: Do not bump major — this is additive and back-compat

verification_steps.step_text: Confirm the version bump and changelog entry are committed and the repo is tagged
verification_steps.expected_result: Tag exists at the new version

tasks.title: Bump stride-marketplace repo version to publish after_goal release
tasks.description: Bump the stride-marketplace repo version so end users picking up the marketplace plugin receive the after_goal-aware skill update. Per the long-standing release rule, the marketplace bump is the releasable step — the stride bump alone is not.
tasks.why: Without the marketplace bump, users in the wild will not see the new hook even though the source repo is updated.
tasks.what: Update version metadata and changelog in stride-marketplace, point at the stride repo's new version.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
stride-marketplace version bumped
Changelog entry mentions after_goal feature pickup
Commit tagged at the new version
Users running /plugin install stride@stride-marketplace pick up the new skill
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Mirror previous coordinated release bumps between stride and stride-marketplace
pitfalls: Do not skip this step — stride bump alone is not user-visible

verification_steps.step_text: From a fresh project, run /plugin install stride@stride-marketplace and verify the new skill version is installed with after_goal documented
